response:
the pen is mightier than bread  
the pen is mightier than cake  
the pen is mightier than butter  

pizzelle stood in her small, cozy donut shop, surrounded by the scent of cinnamon and sugar. she had always believed that kindness was the best policy, and so she made a dozen donuts every day, no matter how few customers came in. some days, she would sell none. some days, just one or two. but she never stopped making them. she believed that someone, someday, would need them.  

but now, the shelves were full. the bins were overflowing. and the donuts were starting to go bad. she had no one to give them to, no one to share with. and the thought of throwing them away made her heart ache. she didn’t want to waste something so sweet, so full of love.  

so she did what she had to do. she ate them. one by one, then two by two, until the shop was empty. and as the days passed, she grew. she filled out, her cheeks rounded, her waist softened, and her smile stayed bright. she didn’t mind. she had always been sweet, and now she was sweet in a different way.  

the pen is mightier than bread  
the pen is mightier than cake  
the pen is mightier than butter
